raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California Broadband for All Bond Act aims to close the digital divide by investing in statewide broadband infrastructure.",
    "The bond measure proposes up to $10 billion in funding to increase access to high-speed internet in unserved and underserved communities throughout California.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted significant disparities in access to reliable, high-speed internet.",
    "Investing in public broadband infrastructure is essential to provide affordable and reliable internet access for all Californians.",
    "There are hundreds of thousands of Californians without internet access, which affects their ability to participate in various economic and educational activities.",
    "High quality internet that is fast, affordable, and reliable is necessary for every home.",
    "Broadband access and digital connectivity are critical for equity in education and economic opportunities.",
    "Reliability of broadband access is directly related to public health, public safety, and economic opportunities.",
    "The transition to distance learning during the pandemic has exacerbated existing inequities in device and internet connectivity.",
    "Addressing broadband connectivity is a priority for California schools to close the digital divide for all students."
  ]
}
```
